signals.title: AUD/USD — RBA Hike + Geopolitical Risk + Retail
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7169 following RBA Governor Bullock's hike to 4.35% and hawkish guidance on inflation persistence. Despite the -35bp carry deficit vs Fed 3.75%, AUD is supported by the active tightening cycle. Retail sentiment is 80% short, diverging from COT LF net long +48,299 (74.8% long), creating a high-conviction contrarian setup. However, surging WTI (+4.39%) and US-Iran ceasefire strain introduce risk-off headwinds that typically favor the USD over high-beta AUD. Sustained break above 0.7200 confirms retail capitulation risk.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short -16,833 (long% 23.0%) shows speculators heavily fading the RBNZ despite a hawkish market bias and a 'Hike' signal for the May 27 meeting. NZD/USD at 0.5873 faces a +150bp carry deficit vs Fed 3.75%, but the extreme spec short positioning creates significant short-squeeze risk if the RBNZ reaffirms the hiking path. Watch for NZD/USD to reclaim 0.5900 on any hawkish RBNZ rhetoric to trigger a long-unwind of USD positions.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Intervention Risk + Carry
signals.text: USD/JPY at 157.24 remains supported by the +300bp Fed-BoJ carry advantage (Fed 3.75% vs BoJ 0.75%). JPY COT LF net short -88,543 (30.6% long) confirms a crowded carry trade. Commerzbank reports Japanese authorities are active near current levels; intervention risk rises significantly as price tests the 160.00 handle. Carry remains the dominant driver while VIX stays at 18.3 (normal volatility), but geopolitical escalation in the Middle East provides a catalyst for rapid JPY safe-haven repatriation.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Correlation Break + Yield Spread
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1693 exhibits a significant correlation break vs EuroStoxx (corr=+0.730 vs norm=-0.138, z=+1.86σ), indicating EUR is decoupling from regional equity weakness. The +175bp USD carry advantage (Fed 3.75% vs ECB 2.0%) structurally limits upside, while COT LF net short -18,293 suggests institutional caution. The bear steepener regime in US yields (+56.6bp 2s10s) supports USD dominance. Watch Swiss CPI today for cross-border EUR/CHF flow implications.
